# EVOLVE-BLOCK-START
"""Optimized shell packing with hexagonal interior for n=26 circles"""
import numpy as np

def construct_packing():
    """
    Optimized packing: corners + edges + hexagonal interior.
    - 4 corners at optimal distance from vertices
    - 12 edge circles with improved spacing
    - 10 interior in true hexagonal pattern (3-2-2-3 rows)
    """
    n = 26
    c = np.zeros((n, 2))
    
    # CORNERS: optimal distance balances corner radius vs edge space
    corner_r = 0.085
    c[0] = [corner_r, corner_r]
    c[1] = [1-corner_r, corner_r]
    c[2] = [corner_r, 1-corner_r]
    c[3] = [1-corner_r, 1-corner_r]
    
    # EDGES: 3 per edge, positioned to avoid corner conflicts
    edge_d = 0.055
    # Bottom
    c[4] = [0.23, edge_d]
    c[5] = [0.50, edge_d]
    c[6] = [0.77, edge_d]
    # Top
    c[7] = [0.23, 1-edge_d]
    c[8] = [0.50, 1-edge_d]
    c[9] = [0.77, 1-edge_d]
    # Left
    c[10] = [edge_d, 0.23]
    c[11] = [edge_d, 0.50]
    c[12] = [edge_d, 0.77]
    # Right
    c[13] = [1-edge_d, 0.23]
    c[14] = [1-edge_d, 0.50]
    c[15] = [1-edge_d, 0.77]
    
    # INTERIOR: hexagonal pattern 3-2-2-3
    # Row 1: 3 circles
    c[16] = [0.28, 0.25]
    c[17] = [0.50, 0.25]
    c[18] = [0.72, 0.25]
    # Row 2: 2 circles (hexagonal offset)
    c[19] = [0.36, 0.42]
    c[20] = [0.64, 0.42]
    # Row 3: 2 circles (hexagonal offset)
    c[21] = [0.36, 0.58]
    c[22] = [0.64, 0.58]
    # Row 4: 3 circles
    c[23] = [0.28, 0.75]
    c[24] = [0.50, 0.75]
    c[25] = [0.72, 0.75]
    
    # Max radii from border constraints
    r = np.array([min(x, y, 1-x, 1-y) for x, y in c])
    
    # Iterative overlap resolution with more passes
    for _ in range(20):
        for i in range(n):
            for j in range(i+1, n):
                d = np.linalg.norm(c[i] - c[j])
                if r[i] + r[j] > d and d > 1e-10:
                    sc = d / (r[i] + r[j])
                    r[i], r[j] = r[i]*sc, r[j]*sc
    
    return c, r, np.sum(r)
# EVOLVE-BLOCK-END


# This part remains fixed (not evolved)
def run_packing():
    """Run the circle packing constructor for n=26"""
    centers, radii, sum_radii = construct_packing()
    return centers, radii, sum_radii


def visualize(centers, radii):
    """
    Visualize the circle packing

    Args:
        centers: np.array of shape (n, 2) with (x, y) coordinates
        radii: np.array of shape (n) with radius of each circle
    """
    import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
    from matplotlib.patches import Circle

    fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(8, 8))

    # Draw unit square
    ax.set_xlim(0, 1)
    ax.set_ylim(0, 1)
    ax.set_aspect("equal")
    ax.grid(True)

    # Draw circles
    for i, (center, radius) in enumerate(zip(centers, radii)):
        circle = Circle(center, radius, alpha=0.5)
        ax.add_patch(circle)
        ax.text(center[0], center[1], str(i), ha="center", va="center")

    plt.title(f"Circle Packing (n={len(centers)}, sum={sum(radii):.6f})")
    plt.show()


if __name__ == "__main__":
    centers, radii, sum_radii = run_packing()
    print(f"Sum of radii: {sum_radii}")
    # AlphaEvolve improved this to 2.635

    # Uncomment to visualize:
    visualize(centers, radii)
